title: Eurya inaequilatera
intro: Eurya inaequilatera, also known as the "Inequilateral Eurya," is a shrub-like plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Eurya inaequilatera include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of ointments and topical creams to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Eurya inaequilatera is a species of flowering plant in the family Pentaphylacaceae. The genus Eurya is a part of the order Ericales.
